title: Library Research Access
transcript: Student: Hi, I'm trying to access the online journal archives for my research paper, but it says my account doesn't have permission. Librarian: Let me check. Are you logging in through the university portal? Student: Yes, but when I click on JSTOR and ScienceDirect, it says access denied. Librarian: Ah, I see the issue. Your library account is set to undergraduate basic access. For senior thesis research, you need extended database access. Student: Oh, I didn't know that. How do I upgrade it? Librarian: You need to fill out a Database Access Request form and have your faculty advisor sign it. Student: Professor Chen is my advisor. Can I email the form to her? Librarian: Yes, but she needs to send it back to us directly from her university email for verification. Once we receive it, the upgrade takes about 24 hours. Student: That's manageable. Is there anything else I should know about the extended access? Librarian: Yes, with extended access you also get interlibrary loan privileges and remote VPN access to specialty databases. Student: Great, that will be really helpful for my research on urban planning trends. Librarian: Good luck with your paper. The form is available on the library website under Student Services.

questions.stem: Why does the student talk to the librarian?
options: To resolve a database access problem for research
To check out books for a literature class
To complain about slow internet in the library
To ask about library hours during finals week
questions.explanation: The student cannot access online journal archives and visits the librarian to resolve the permission issue.

questions.stem: What is causing the student's access problem?
options: The account only has undergraduate basic access
The university portal is currently down
The student forgot the login password
The databases are restricted to graduate students
questions.explanation: The librarian states the student's library account is set to undergraduate basic access, which lacks the needed database permissions.

audioSegments.text: Hi, I'm trying to access the online journal archives for my research paper, but it says my account doesn't have permission.
Let me check. Are you logging in through the university portal?
Yes, but when I click on JSTOR and ScienceDirect, it says access denied.
Ah, I see the issue. Your library account is set to undergraduate basic access. For senior thesis research, you need extended database access.
Oh, I didn't know that. How do I upgrade it?
You need to fill out a Database Access Request form and have your faculty advisor sign it.
Professor Chen is my advisor. Can I email the form to her?
Yes, but she needs to send it back to us directly from her university email for verification. Once we receive it, the upgrade takes about 24 hours.
That's manageable. Is there anything else I should know about the extended access?
Yes, with extended access you also get interlibrary loan privileges and remote VPN access to specialty databases.
Great, that will be really helpful for my research on urban planning trends.
Good luck with your paper. The form is available on the library website under Student Services.
